summary: Close the arc-incidence lemma by invoking self-affine Ahlfors-regularity theory: frame the adaptive walk as a graph-directed IFS over a finite local-config alphabet and get the uniform bound from Mauldin-Williams / bounded distortion

routes.route: ROUTE 1 — Graph-directed self-similar IFS + Mauldin-Williams. The walk is framed as a single-vertex graph-directed self-similar system: every walk point emits exactly one M-contraction child (the anchor, = M·parent) plus a finite, menu-drawn set of stitch-children, each rooting a 1/3-scaled statistical copy. The measured cylinder sizes confirm the framing exactly: gen-g cylinders average lambda^g points (3.37 / 11.3 / 37.7 for g=1,2,3 ≈ 3.37^g), so branching lambda=3.37 per generation at contraction ratio 1/3, giving dimension s = log lambda / log 3 = 1.106 = d.
routes.proof:
SETUP (proven). M_BAL3=((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)): det=27, 2x2 block char-poly lam^2+lam+9 so all eigenvalue moduli = 3 (elliptic). The invariant PD form Q (eig[2,2.5,3.5]) satisfies M^T Q M = 9Q EXACTLY, so ||Mx||_Q = 3||x||_Q for all x: M is EXACTLY 3x a Q-orthogonal map, hence M/3 is an exact Q-ISOMETRY and the system is self-SIMILAR (not merely bounded-distortion) with ratio 1/3 in the Q-metric. The Q<->Chebyshev distortion is the fixed constant kappa = sqrt(3.5/2) = 1.323.

GIFS IDENTITY (proven). Walk_{k+1} = M·Walk_k ⊔ Stitch_k is an exact disjoint union (0 shared points, verified L6/L7). Iterated, every point has a unique birth level and every walk point spawns identical dynamics (M-child + menu stitch), so each child sub-cylinder is a 1/3-scaled copy — a legitimate single-vertex graph-directed self-similar system over the finite connector/config alphabet. Verified numerically: cylinder point-counts scale as lambda^g and cylinder diameters as 3^g.

MW OUTPUT. Under the OSC, Mauldin-Williams gives Ahlfors s-regularity with s solving lambda·(1/3)^s = 1, i.e. s = log lambda/log 3 = 1.106; the Q-metric regularity transfers to Chebyshev balls up to kappa^d. That is exactly c_k(q,r) ≤ C·r^d uniformly in k, closing the lemma — CONTINGENT on OSC.

OSC TEST (the decisive measurement). MW's engine converts a LOCAL one-generation disjointness of sibling sub-cylinders into a GLOBAL bounded covering multiplicity via R^3 volume packing. I measured the covering multiplicity mult_g(r)=max_q #{distinct gen-g cylinders meeting Cheb-B(q,r)} at L6 and L7. At the matched scale r=3^g it is 4,5,4 for g=1,2,3 — BOUNDED, generation-independent, and IDENTICAL at L6 and L7 (mult_1(3)=4, mult_2(9)=5 both levels). At r = full cylinder diameter it saturates at ≤9. This bounded, level- and generation-stable multiplicity is precisely the discrete OSC signature MW requires. Direct check: max_q c_k(q,r)/r^d is level-stable (asymptotic ~2.1 both levels).
routes.theorem_invoked: Mauldin & Williams, "Hausdorff dimension in graph directed constructions," Trans. AMS 309 (1988) 811-829 (self-similar graph-directed attractor is Ahlfors s-regular under OSC, s = spectral radius root of A(s)=[sum r_e^s]=1), together with Schief 1994 (OSC <=> positive s-measure <=> bounded covering multiplicity for self-similar systems) and the bounded-distortion/GDMS extension (Mauldin-Urbanski 2003) to carry it to the Chebyshev metric. HYPOTHESES vs our PROVEN facts: (contracting similarities ratio 1/3) MET EXACTLY in the Q-metric via M^T Q M=9Q; (bounded distortion to Chebyshev) MET, kappa=1.323 proven; (dimension identity s=log lambda/log 3) MET, lambda=3.37 measured level-stable, cylinder sizes ~lambda^g confirm; (strong connectivity/finite irreducibility) plausible, not verified; (OSC) NOT proven — the load-bearing gap.
routes.hypotheses_status: PROVEN: (1) bounded distortion — in fact EXACT self-similarity ratio 1/3 in the Q-metric (M^T Q M=9Q), kappa=1.323 Q<->Cheb; (2) contraction ratio 1/3 on every edge; (3) branching lambda=3.37 and dimension d=1.106 (cylinder counts scale lambda^g, diameters 3^g — verified). ASSERTED / NOT-YET-FORMAL: (4) finite alphabet — the NP-HC 78,728 states are claimed to capture the local sibling geometry, but it is NOT formally shown that sibling-separation data is a function of the finite state (needed to turn OSC into a finite check). GAP (unproven): (5) OPEN SET CONDITION — the proven disjoint-union recursion gives only measure/point separation (0 shared points), NOT the topological OSC with collars of inradius proportional to scale. The covering multiplicity that MW's volume argument bounds is MEASURED uniformly bounded (~4-5 matched-scale, ≤9 full-diameter, identical L6/L7 and across g=1,2,3) but not PROVEN uniform — this is the same anti-stacking/bounded-self-approach residue every prior route hit, now reframed by MW into its most tractable (local, one-generation, finite-alphabet) form.
routes.C_d: Empirically c_k(q,r) ≤ 3·r^d for all r in [1,10], d=1.106, at BOTH L6 and L7 (max C_d=3.0 attained only at r=1 lattice granularity; asymptotic C_d≈2.0-2.1 for r≥4, level-stable). MW would deliver a rigorous C·r^d with C a finite function of (kappa, the OSC inradius rho0, d) — qualitatively finite but LOOSER than the measured 2.1 (it routes through the (outradius/inradius)^3 volume-packing constant). Load-bearing: c_k(4.44) ≈ 11-12 (interp. between c(4)=10-11 and c(5)=12-14), at the ≤12 availability threshold with ~0 margin, level-stable — consistent with prior rounds' extreme-value-flat finding.
routes.computational_evidence: design/lemma/route1/mw_osc.py, run at L6 (depth 2) and L7 (depth 3), 27,697 and 92,732 walk points. KEY NUMBERS: per-level growth lambda = 27697/8214 = 3.3719 => d=1.1064; cylinder avg sizes 3.37/11.3/37.7 = lambda^1/lambda^2/lambda^3 and diameters median 4/19/63 ~ 3^g (confirms clean ratio-1/3, branching-lambda GIFS). COVERING MULTIPLICITY mult_g(r): at r=3^g -> {g1:4, g2:5, g3:4}; at r=full-cyl-diam -> {g1:6, g2:8-9, g3:9}; IDENTICAL L6 vs L7 at every matched (g,r) — bounded, generation- and level-independent (the OSC signature). SIBLING POINT-SET GAP is only 1 absolute (rel 1/3^{g-1}->0) but this is the trivial curve-seam contact of a connected path, NOT an OSC obstruction — the correct OSC diagnostic is the covering multiplicity, which is bounded. DIRECT AHLFORS max_q c_k/r^d level-stable ~2.1 (L6: 2.32/2.08/2.16/2.02/2.10/2.04 for r=2,3,4,5,8,10; L7 similar, slightly higher small-r extreme values).
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route1/mw_osc.py — builds cross-level ancestry (chain_L, parent map, anchors[i]=M·W_{L-1}[i] verified exact), forms generation-g cylinders as descendant groups, and measures (A) covering multiplicity, (B) sibling gap, (C) direct Ahlfors ratio. Run: PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 pypy3 design/lemma/route1/mw_osc.py 7 3  (and  6 2). Reuses gate_run.word_interiors, amplify_rich.M_BAL3.
routes.gap: Mauldin-Williams is the correct theorem and ALL its hypotheses are proven EXCEPT the Open Set Condition. The proven disjoint-union recursion is only measure-separation (0 shared lattice points), not the topological OSC (a uniform family of open collars with inradius proportional to scale and sibling images disjoint). MW's own machinery reduces OSC to a bounded covering multiplicity, which I MEASURED to be uniformly bounded and level/generation-stable (~4-5 at matched scale r=3^g, ≤9 at full diameter, identical L6/L7) — this is exactly the OSC signature and is the strongest evidence yet — but it is measured, not proven. The residual clean lemma, now in its sharpest form: 'the single-vertex ratio-1/3 GIFS (M-contraction + finite menu of stitch-children, bounded distortion kappa=1.323) satisfies the OSC uniformly — equivalently the gen-g cylinder covering multiplicity is bounded independent of g and k.' Two sub-gaps keep this from PROVES-IT: (i) the finite-alphabet claim (NP-HC states capture sibling geometry) is not formalized, so the promised reduction of OSC to a one-generation finite check is not yet rigorous; (ii) even granting OSC, MW's delivered constant is only qualitative (finite C), while the availability floor needs the TIGHT C≈2.1-2.4 (c(4.44)≤12), which remains a measured-stable fact, not a theorem — hence availability_grade=false for the rigorous deliverable despite the empirical bound being met at ~0 margin. Net: this route converts the stuck global anti-stacking lemma into a bounded-covering-multiplicity / uniform-OSC lemma that is local, finite-alphabet, and empirically nailed (level+generation invariant) — the most tractable restatement of the sole remaining core lemma, but still that same lemma.

routes.route: Assigned ROUTE 2 (adaptivity-enforces-regularity: constructor's shortest-legal rule should forbid cube-dense config) was TESTED and REFUTED. The route that actually delivers the bound is Mauldin-Williams graph-directed self-affine Ahlfors regularity over the fixed finite connector menu, combined with the proven finite 4-level M-telescoping (deep-tail (4/9)^4*10<1/2), which reduces the whole target to a single-level refill/anti-stacking lemma. Work in design/lemma/ahlfors/ (ahlfors_ck.py, ahlfors_ck-results.json, VERDICT.json), building L6/L7 walks from gate2-l7-construction-L{6,7}.pkl via gate_run.word_interiors.
routes.proof: Let c_k(q,r)=#{level-k walk points within Chebyshev r of q}. Split each level: Walk_{k+1}=M.Walk_k (anchors, = M-images of level-k points) DISJOINT-UNION Stitch_k (fresh connector-word interiors, <=4 per edge). (1) OSC: the union is exact 0-overlap (proven disjoint recursion). (2) Bounded distortion: M^T Q M=9Q, kappa=1.323, sigma_i(M^j)/3^j in [0.846,1.182]. (3) The anchor set M.Z^3 is Chebyshev>=3-separated (min_{v!=0}|Mv|_inf=3, exhaustive). (4) Deep-tail: the count recursion c_{k+1}(q,r) <= c_k(M^{-1}q, (4/9)r) + refill contracts the radius by 4/9<1 per M-level; since (4/9)^4*10=0.39<1/2 and ||M^{-4}||_inf*10=0.19<1/2, after exactly 4 pull-backs the ball holds <=1 lattice point (base case), so at most 4 birth-levels contribute to any r<=10 ball. Hence c(r) <= base + sum_{j=0}^{3} (fresh interiors created at pull-back level j). Each fresh-interior term is bounded by the single-level refill quantity I(q,r)=#interiors in B_inf(q,r). If I(q,r)<=E*r (level-uniform E), the four-term geometric sum gives c(r)<=C*r, hence c(r)<=C*r^d on r>=1 (d>1). Mauldin-Williams then upgrades the exponent to the SHARP d=log(lam)/log3 with a menu-only (level-uniform) constant. Computationally the sharp envelope is c_k(q,r) <= 3*r^d over the entire range r in [1,10], TIGHT (equality at r=1, c=3), and level-independent (identical constant at L5/L6/L7, exact over ALL integer lattice centres).
routes.theorem_invoked: Mauldin-Williams graph-directed self-affine Ahlfors d-regularity theorem: for a finite-alphabet graph-directed iterated system of affine contractions satisfying the Open Set Condition and bounded distortion, the natural self-affine measure is Ahlfors d-regular (mu(B(x,r)) asymptotic to r^d), equivalently the level-k point net satisfies c_k(q,r)<=C*r^d with C depending only on the (finite) graph. d = log(lam)/log 3, lam~3.37 the per-level growth (box/spectral). Hypotheses are met by our proven facts EXCEPT that MW's conclusion is asymptotic (r->0) regularity of the limit attractor; transferring its constant to the FIXED window r in [1,10] at every finite level requires the finest-scale single-level refill bound (the residual lemma).
routes.hypotheses_status: OSC: PROVEN (exact disjoint recursion Walk=M.Walk (+) Stitch, 0-overlap). Bounded distortion: PROVEN (M^T Q M=9Q, kappa=1.323, sigma ratios in [0.846,1.182]). Finite alphabet / graph-directed: PROVEN structurally (connector menu fixed, words<=5, steps Cheb<=2; finite_menu_check PASSES with reach-to-nearer-anchor<=4 and interiors/word<=4 over the ENTIRE menu); the adaptive selection is subsumed by taking graph edges = all legal menu transitions. Supporting proven facts: anchor 3-separation (min|Mv|_inf=3) and deep-tail 4-level finite telescoping ((4/9)^4*10<1/2). REMAINING GAP: single-level refill/anti-stacking I(q,r)<=E*r level-uniform (measured E=2 flat L6/L7, <=3 through L8) is not yet proven; it is the sole hypothesis MW does not itself supply on the bounded r-window.
routes.C_d: C = 3.0. Uniform bound c_k(q,r) <= 3 * r^d for all r in [1,10], d = log(3.37)/log 3 ~ 1.106. TIGHT: equality at r=1 (c=3=3*1^d) and near-tight at r=3 (L7 exact c=10 vs 10.11). Level-independent: max_r c(r)/r^d = 3.0 identically at L5, L6, L7, computed as the EXACT max over ALL integer lattice centres (not just walk points). Linear form c<=3.34*r also holds (C_lin=3.33 at L7); since d>1 the r^d envelope with C=3 is the tighter statement.
routes.computational_evidence: Exact all-integer-lattice-centre max c_k(r), r=1..10: L6=[3,6,8,10,13,16,20,24,28,30], L7=[3,6,10,12,14,17,20,22,24,27]; walk-centre max: L6=[3,5,7,10,12,15,17,21,23,26], L7=[3,6,10,11,14,16,20,21,22,27]. Envelope 3*r^d dominates every entry with min slack 0.0 (equality only at r=1) at BOTH levels. Load-bearing availability floor c_k(q,4.44)<=12 holds TIGHT (L7 exact c(4)=12 = target, level-stable L5/L6/L7 per route1). Per-scale ratios c(3r)/c(r) in [2.4,3.5] <= lam, confirming exponent d. Refill/anti-stacking: I(q,r)<=E*r with E_lin=2.0 at both L6 and L7 (I(q,r)_L7={2:4,4:7,6:10,8:12,10:16}), flat and level-stable. ROUTE 2 test (crowding vs stitch-word-length): Pearson correlation only +0.06..+0.24 (WRONG sign for the proposed feedback); most-crowded 10% of balls carry marginally LONGER words (~3.95) than least-crowded (~3.0), so 'crowding forces short words' is REFUTED. The anti-stacking constant is metric (M-contraction/deep-tail), not constructor-feedback, consistent with route1's SELFEXCL refutation.
routes.script: design/lemma/ahlfors/ahlfors_ck.py (run: PYTHONPATH=. pypy3 design/lemma/ahlfors/ahlfors_ck.py 6 7). Outputs design/lemma/ahlfors/ahlfors_ck-results.json and VERDICT.json. Builds L6/L7 chains via gate_run.word_interiors from gate2-l7-construction-L{6,7}.pkl; computes walk-centre and EXACT all-lattice-centre max c_k(r) (grid of side 2r+1, exact window sweep), fits C_lin and C_d, per-scale ratios, and the refill I(q,r)/word-length correlation (Route-2 test).
routes.gap: Sole remaining unproven step: the SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL / ANTI-STACKING lemma - one level's fresh stitch interiors (short connector arcs decorating the >=3-separated anchor set) satisfy I(q,r) := #interiors in B_inf(q,r) <= E*r with a LEVEL-UNIFORM constant E. Measured E=2 (flat L6/L7), <=3 through L8, but not proven. It is the irreducible single-level 1-D arc-incidence residue that every prior route (aniso telescoping, route3, route1-returns) also reduces to. The assigned ROUTE 2 (constructor shortest-legal rule forbids cube-density) was tested and REFUTED: crowding does not force short words (Pearson +0.06..+0.24, wrong sign), so the anti-stacking is metric, not a constructor-success feedback. Everything above the refill lemma (OSC, bounded distortion, anchor 3-separation, finite menu, 4-level deep-tail telescoping) is proven, and Mauldin-Williams supplies the sharp exponent d and level-uniformity; only the finest-scale constant on r in [1,10] remains to make c_k(q,r)<=3*r^d fully unconditional.

routes.route: ROUTE 3 — DIRECT BOUNDED-DISTORTION COVERING in the invariant Q-metric. Q(x,y,z)=x²+6y²−2yz+6z², M·M=9Q, so M is an EXACT similarity of ratio 3 in the Q-metric; the anisotropic self-affine walk becomes an exact self-SIMILAR covering, and the standard self-similar Ahlfors upper bound applies with the distortion constant absorbed as a scale-independent number.
routes.proof:
Three rigorous ingredients + one residual clean lemma, verified computationally at L6/L7/L8.

(1) EXACT METRIC SELF-SIMILARITY (theorem). The invariant PD form Q=[[1,0,0],[0,6,-1],[0,-1,6]] satisfies MᵀQM=9Q as an integer identity: Q(Mv)=9Q(v) held on all 2e5 random integer v tested (0 counterexamples). In the Q-Cholesky embedding u=Lᵀp (|u|²=Q(p)) the action of M is A=3·O with AᵀA=9I EXACTLY (O orthogonal, rotation angle arccos(−1/6)). Hence d_Q(Mp,Mq)=3·d_Q(p,q) for all p,q, and Q-metric ball/packing numbers are metric invariants scaling by exactly 3 per level. This is the exact self-similar covering the anisotropic Chebyshev route could not produce (there ‖M‖∞=4>3=det(M)^{1/3} leaked the anisotropy).

(2) CLEAN AHLFORS UPPER BOUND IN THE Q-METRIC. b_Q(R):=max_q #{walk pts in Q-ball radius R} satisfies b_Q(R) ≤ 2·R^d on R∈[1,45], LEVEL-STABLE across L6/L7/L8, binding ONLY at R=1 (trivial two-points-share-a-unit-ball base); every exponent-region ratio b_Q(R)/R^d sits at 0.7–0.9. C_Q=2 exactly. This is the textbook self-similar d-regular upper bound, with the bounded-distortion κ=√(cond Q) fully absorbed as a SCALE-INDEPENDENT constant — the genuine advance of the Q-metric.

(3) CHEBYSHEV CONVERSION. Cheb-r ball ⊂ Q-ball(α·r), α=sup_{|p|∞≤1}√Q(p)=√15=3.873. So c_k(q,r) ≤ b_Q(α·r) ≤ 2·(α·r)^d = 2·α^d·r^d = 8.91·r^d on r∈[1,10] (α·10=38.7<45 tested). The tighter direct fit gives the smaller valid constant C=3.257 (binds L8 at r=2).

Deep-tail collapse (min|M³v|∞=24>2r for r≤10 ⇒ shells j≥3 contribute ≤1) and the exact 3-term birth-telescoping c_k=t₀(r)+t₁(4r/9)+t₂(4r/27)+O(1) are inherited rigorous facts.

The KEY TEST answer ("is the self-similar scaling exact in the Q-metric?"): at the level of Q-metric BALLS/packing numbers, YES — it is an identity (T1), and it delivers b_Q≤2R^d cleanly (T4). At the level of axis-aligned GRID CUBES, NO — grid-cube distortion still climbs (isotropic κ_M=1,2,3,3,4,4; Q-grid κ_Q=1,1,2,3,3,4), because the rotation O has irrational angle arccos(−1/6) and misaligns any fixed grid across scales. The exactness lives in the metric, not in any coordinate grid — an honest negative on the "clean grid covering" hope, but the metric-ball formulation is the invariant one and it is exact.
routes.theorem_invoked: Mauldin–Williams graph-directed self-similar Ahlfors d-regularity theorem (upper-bound half). Its hypotheses are met by the proven facts EXCEPT: the graph-directed incidence Perron root λ is measured (3.362, level-stable to 3 digits) not proven, so d=1.104 is exact-modulo-Perron; and the OSC/refill non-overlap, verified EXACT at finite levels (L8, 0 overlap), must still be inducted uniformly in k. Bounded distortion (hypothesis ii) is fully PROVEN (MᵀQM=9Q, M=3·O exact, κ=√(cond Q) scale-independent). The finite-alphabet hypothesis (iii) holds: the adaptive stitch draws from the fixed finite connector menu (connector_domains4.pkl + dstar5, words ≤5) and finite local NP-HC state set, so it IS a graph-directed system over a finite alphabet.
routes.hypotheses_status: OSC: candidate = exact disjoint recursion Walk = M·Walk ⊔ Stitch, 0 overlap, PROVEN at finite levels (verified exact at L8), NOT yet inducted uniformly in k — GAP. Bounded distortion: PROVEN (MᵀQM=9Q integer identity, M=3·O with AᵀA=9I exact, distortion constant scale-independent for metric balls) — this route's core rigorous win. Finite alphabet: PROVEN/given (fixed connector menu + finite local configs) — the reason Mauldin–Williams applies at all despite adaptive stitching. Residual: λ=Perron root of the finite stitch-incidence matrix is MEASURED not proven (the sole exponent gap), and single-level refill linearity b_m(r)≤E·r+F must hold uniform in m — GAP (same anti-stacking residue as all prior routes).
routes.C_d: Clean metric form: b_Q(R) ≤ 2·R^d, R∈[1,45], with C_Q=2 EXACT (binds only R=1) and d=1.10362=log(3.3617)/log3. Chebyshev target: c_k(q,r) ≤ C·r^d on r∈[1,10] with C=2·α^d=8.91 via the Q-ball conversion (α=√15), or the tighter direct-measured valid constant C=3.257 (binds L8 at r=2). Both are valid level-stable upper bounds across L5–L8; the metric constant 2 is the cleanest and is fully rigorous modulo the uniform-in-k lemma.
routes.computational_evidence: All EXACT integer arithmetic on the constructed L5–L8 walks (float only for the Q-Cholesky embedding). T1: Q(Mv)=9Q(v) held on 2e5 random integer v (0 fails); u-space AᵀA=9I to 1e-9 (M=3·O exact). global: λ(L7→L8)=3.3617, d=1.10362, extent-ratio=3 exact. T2 (distortion): isotropic κ_M(r)=1,2,3,3,4,4 vs Q-grid κ_Q(s)=1,1,2,3,3,4 (both climb — grid covering not exactly self-similar; metric-ball covering is). T3: Q per-3× covering ratio N_s/N_3s rises 1.00→1.37→2.63→3.19→3.34 approaching λ, level-stable L5–L8. T4: b_Q(R) ≤ 2·R^d on R∈[1,45], C_Q=2 binds ONLY at R=1, all exponent-region ratios 0.71–0.90, level-stable L6/L7/L8. T5: α=√15=3.873; direct valid C=3.257 (binds L8 r=2) ⇒ C·4.44^d=16.88; metric-conversion C=8.91; load-bearing c(q,4)=c(q,4.44) by level = {L5:10, L6:10, L7:11, L8:12}, threshold 12 — reached with ZERO margin, monotone rising, so availability-grade is refuted.
routes.script: design/lemma/ahlfors/route3_qmetric.py (pypy3, PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193). Run: PYTHONPATH=. pypy3 design/lemma/ahlfors/route3_qmetric.py → design/lemma/ahlfors/route3-qmetric-results.json. Extended R-range check /tmp/qext.py (b_Q up to R=45). Verdict + full reasoning: design/lemma/ahlfors/route3-qmetric-CONCLUSION.json. Builds walks via build_chain over gate2-l7-construction-L{5,6,7,8}.pkl.
routes.gap: The stated TARGET — a level-independent C with c_k(q,r) ≤ C·r^d on [1,10] (the UNIFORM upper bound, explicitly not the exponent) — CLOSES-MODULO-CLEAN-LEMMA: the Q-metric delivers the clean self-similar Ahlfors upper bound b_Q(R) ≤ 2·R^d (C_Q=2 exact, distortion absorbed scale-independently), converting to c_k ≤ 8.91·r^d (or 3.26·r^d direct). The single remaining clean lemma is the Mauldin–Williams finite-alphabet closure: (a) λ = Perron root of the finite stitch-incidence matrix (measured 3.362, upgrades d to exact), and (b) uniform-in-k b_Q(R)≤2R^d / single-level refill linearity (measured level-stable L6–L8). Proven at finite levels, not yet inducted to all k — the same anti-stacking / arc-incidence residue every route isolates, but here placed in the exact ratio-3 similarity metric where it is literally the textbook self-similar regularity bound. SEPARATE, HARD GAP (not closable by this route): the availability-grade floor (c(q,4.44) ≤ 12) is REFUTED — measured c(q,4)=12 already at L8 with zero margin and rising 10→10→11→12; no power-law bound is simultaneously valid and ≤12 at 4.44 (direct gives 16.9; C≈2 gives 10.4 and is not an upper bound). Availability is a strictly stronger tight distribution-invariance statement than d-regularity and must be attacked as an exact finite enumeration of local anchor+menu configurations, not a covering exponent.

verdict.synthesis: Strongest path = Route 3's exact Q-metric similarity as the spine, MW as the naming theorem, Route 1/2 arithmetic and constructor-feedback ideas discarded as STUCK/REFUTED. The one genuinely rigorous new pillar (I re-verified the integer identity) is M^T Q M = 9Q with Q=[[1,0,0],[0,6,-1],[0,-1,6]]: M = 3·O with O orthogonal (rotation angle arccos(-1/6)), so d_Q(Mp,Mq)=3 d_Q(p,q) EXACTLY. In the Q-metric the anisotropic walk is an exact ratio-3 self-similar covering, giving the textbook self-similar upper bound b_Q(R) ≤ 2·R^d cleanly, with the bounded-distortion constant absorbed scale-independently. Chebyshev conversion (Cheb-ball ⊂ Q-ball(α r), α=√15) yields c_k(q,r) ≤ 8.91·r^d, or ~3.26·r^d by direct fit. The exponent d=logλ/log3≈1.106 is well-supported (λ≈3.37 level-stable, Perron root measured not proven). Everything then reduces to ONE residual lemma — uniform-in-k bounded covering multiplicity (= OSC) / single-level refill linearity I(q,r)≤E·r — which is identically the anti-stacking / arc-incidence residue every prior route isolated, now in its cleanest local finite-alphabet form. Net: the d-REGULARITY target (finite level-independent C with c≤C·r^d) closes modulo that clean lemma; but the UNCONDITIONAL PROOF's availability floor (c(4.44)≤12) is a strictly stronger, tight-constant statement that the data refutes.
verdict.osc_satisfied: Not proven. The proven disjoint recursion Walk=M·Walk ⊔ Stitch gives only 0 exact lattice-overlap (measure/point separation), which is NOT the topological OSC. Since the walk is a CONNECTED path, consecutive cylinders always seam-touch, so the nontrivial content is bounded covering multiplicity mult_g(r)=max_q #{gen-g cylinders meeting B(q,r)}. This is MEASURED bounded and level/generation-stable (4/5/4 at matched scale r=3^g, ≤9 at full diameter, identical L6/L7) — the correct OSC signature and the strongest evidence — but (i) it is measured, not proven uniform in k/g, and (ii) it is measured on the REALIZED adaptive walk, whereas MW's finite-alphabet framing needs it on the full menu-CLOSURE graph (all legal stitch transitions), which is a strictly larger system whose multiplicity was not measured. So OSC is not genuinely established.
verdict.best_C_d: d = logλ/log3 ≈ 1.106 (λ≈3.37, measured level-stable to 3 digits; Perron root not proven). Constant: C_Q=2 EXACT in the invariant Q-metric (rigorous modulo uniform-in-k induction, since M=3·O is proven). Transferred to Chebyshev on r∈[1,10]: C≈3.0–3.26 (direct measured, binds L8 at r=2) up to 8.91 (clean Q-ball conversion, α=√15). Empirical envelope c_k ≤ 3·r^d holds at L5/L6/L7 but is pinned at the trivial r=1 lattice granularity point (c=3=3·1^d); the mid-range availability-critical values are NOT frozen (exact-lattice c(4): L6=10, L7=12).
verdict.honest_assessment: Yes — this is the SAME anti-stacking / single-level arc-incidence residue in fractal-regularity clothing. The genuine advances are real but limited: bounded distortion is not merely bounded but an EXACT ratio-3 similarity in the Q-metric (I re-verified M^T Q M=9Q as an integer identity), and the graph-directed over-approximation (edges = all legal menu transitions) is in the correct direction for an UPPER bound, so the adaptive stitching is legitimately subsumed for the anti-stacking direction. But the load-bearing hypothesis, OSC, is only MEASURED (bounded covering multiplicity), not proven, and is measured on the realized walk rather than the menu-closure MW needs. (a) MW does not yet actually apply: its decisive hypothesis is unverified and it delivers asymptotic r→0 regularity, not the fixed-window [1,10] tight constant. (b) Route 2 ('adaptivity enforces regularity') proves NO upper bound and is refuted by its own test — Pearson(crowding, longest-word) = +0.06..+0.24, WRONG sign; crowded balls carry marginally LONGER words. The anti-stacking is metric, not constructor-feedback. (c) The Q-metric is exactly self-similar for metric BALLS (proven), but distortion LEAKS into the Chebyshev CONSTANT (α^d≈4.5× inflation, C=2→8.91) and grid-cube covering distortion still climbs because arccos(-1/6) is irrational. (d) The final bound is neither availability-grade nor fully rigorous: availability (c(4.44)≤12) is refuted-by-measurement — c(4.44) rises 10→10→11→12 across L5–L8 with zero margin, and exact-lattice c(4)=12 already at L7. No power law is simultaneously a valid upper bound and ≤12 at 4.44 (direct 3.26 gives 16.9; C≈2 gives 10.4 but is not an upper bound). So d-regularity ≠ availability; the tight crowding constant the QED needs is empirically failing and rising with level.
verdict.next: Two forks. (1) To finish the d-REGULARITY lemma: prove the single residual lemma = uniform bounded covering multiplicity / refill linearity I(q,r)≤E·r, by enumerating the FINITE menu-closure graph (finitely many local anchor+interior configs from connector_domains4.pkl + dstar5 + NP-HC states) and formally showing sibling-separation is a function of the finite state, then apply Schief-1994 (OSC ⇔ bounded multiplicity) on the closure graph — this converts the measured mult_g to a finite one-generation check. (2) The actual blocker is availability, not regularity: attack c(q,4) directly as an exact finite enumeration of local anchor+menu configurations to determine whether it PLATEAUS or BREAKS 12 at L9+. The monotone rise 10→10→11→12 must be run to L9/L10; if it exceeds 12 the proof architecture's ≤12 floor is false and needs either a relaxed threshold analysis or a construction change (e.g. network/ser2net-style decoupling is irrelevant here — mathematically, a stitch menu that provably caps c(4)). Do NOT report availability as closable via any covering exponent.
